campbell.title: Campbell City Council — March 24, 2026
campbell.summary: Campbell's City Council met on March 24, 2026, to honor K9 Lucas, the police department's first working dog who recently passed away. The council also approved updates to the city's beekeeping rules, discussed a new state law about development near transit stations, and reviewed a study about creating homeless services in the West Valley region.
keyTopics: K9 Lucas memorial and recognition
Updated beekeeping ordinance approved
Senate Bill 79 transit-oriented development hearing (3 council members recused due to conflicts)
West Valley Homeless Services Feasibility Study and regional task force recommendation
Fiscal Year 2025 financial report accepted

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 31, 2026
saratoga.summary: The city council met with five commissions to review and approve their plans and priorities for the next fiscal year. The commissions covered topics ranging from preserving historic buildings and supporting the library to managing parks, improving traffic safety, and reviewing development projects. The council also discussed a legal matter in a private session.
keyTopics: Historic preservation priorities for Saratoga
Library and public art initiatives
Parks and recreation programs and facility improvements
Traffic safety improvements and road projects
Planning and development review process

los-altos.title: Los Altos City Council — March 24, 2026
los-altos.summary: The Los Altos City Council met on March 24, 2026 to handle routine business including approving previous meeting minutes and the treasurer's report. They also adopted policies on weed control completion, city council procedures, affordable housing unit transfers, and approved raising Pride and Juneteenth flags around the city.
keyTopics: Weed abatement program completion report
Affordable housing (BMR) unit transfer policy changes
Pride and Juneteenth flag raising approvals
Santa Clara Valley Transportation Authority presentation
Housing element implementation schedule discussion

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — March 17, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on March 17, 2026, to approve meeting minutes, review the city's finances from January, and move forward on several projects including a new housing development called Solana Project. The council also discussed plans for community health services and made adjustments to how they document their meetings.
keyTopics: Solana Project housing development approved for construction
January 2026 city finances and investments reviewed
General Plan and Housing Element progress report accepted
2026 community health and senior services work plan approved
Changes made to how city council meetings are recorded and formatted

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 1, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting on April 1, 2026 to prepare for upcoming council meetings scheduled for April 7 and April 14. The council reviewed what items would be on each agenda, decided whether any items needed specific time slots for discussion, and checked if any documents required special notice procedures.
keyTopics: Finalized the April 7, 2026 meeting agenda
Planned items for the April 14, 2026 draft agenda
Set time slots for specific agenda items as needed
Reviewed public records from March 19-26, 2026
Checked waiver requirements for documents and agenda items

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — March 30, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a meeting on March 30, 2026, where they approved previous meeting minutes and opened the floor for residents to share comments and concerns. The council also reviewed committee reports and discussed upcoming meetings for various city boards and commissions.
keyTopics: Approval of previous meeting minutes
Public comment period for residents
Committee and staff reports
Schedules for upcoming board and commission meetings
Recreation Division contact for agenda questions (650-903-6331)

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — March 25, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission met to review applications for federal grant money that helps fund affordable housing and community programs in Sunnyvale. The commission approved meeting notes from the previous month and heard presentations from organizations seeking funding to support housing and human services.
keyTopics: Federal grant funding applications for housing and community programs
Review of federal Community Development Block Grant (CDBG) money available for local projects
Approval of previous meeting minutes from February

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — March 27, 2026
cupertino.summary: The city council focused on ways to support local businesses and encourage shopping in Cupertino. They discussed a program to help businesses catch up on license fees without penalties, plus plans to promote local shopping through community events and partnerships with private companies.
keyTopics: Business License Amnesty Program - helping businesses get current on fees without penalties
Shopping center outreach - working directly with local malls and shopping areas
Summer of Celebrations Shop Local Campaign - promoting local businesses through community events
Public-private partnerships - getting private companies to sponsor city events
Economic incentives for startups and retail - attracting new and small businesses to Cupertino

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 2, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara held a Historical and Landmarks Commission hearing on April 2, 2026, where the city considered matters related to historic buildings and landmarks in the area. Residents could attend either in person or join remotely via Zoom to watch and participate in the discussion.
keyTopics: Historical and Landmarks Commission hearing
Hybrid meeting format (in-person and Zoom)
Public comment submission deadline at noon on meeting day
Email submission to PlanningPublicComment@santaclaraca.gov

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — March 17, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council held a routine meeting to handle administrative housekeeping items like approving previous meeting minutes and setting the calendar for future meetings. The council also approved Mayor Montano's travel to a 2026 wellness conference and confirmed a school district representative appointment.
keyTopics: Approved City Council meeting minutes and updated meeting schedule
Mayor Montano authorized to attend WELL 2026 Annual conference
Confirmed new Milpitas Unified School District representative to Economic committee
Reviewed upcoming agenda items requested by council members

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 23, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met to celebrate Women's History Month and approve two important agreements. They authorized the city to partner with the American Red Cross to use city facilities during emergencies, and approved a three-year contract to purchase email security software (Mimecast) for about $93,400 per year.
keyTopics: Women's History Month proclamation
Disaster relief agreement with American Red Cross
Email security software contract ($280,000 total over 3 years)
Late agenda items added to discussion
